content.problemStatement:
After learning about the bitwise AND operation, Ridbit challenged Ashishgup to solve the following problem.

Let's denote the bitwise AND of one or more integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aka_1, a_2, \ldots, a_ka1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ak‚Äã by AND(a1,a2,‚Ä¶,ak)AND(a_1, a_2, \ldots, a_k)AND(a1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,ak‚Äã). For example, AND(7,3,5)=1AND(7, 3, 5) = 1AND(7,3,5)=1. In particular, for an integer xxx, AND(x)=xAND(x) = xAND(x)=x.

Given a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã, Ridbit wants Ashishgup to reorder its elements (possibly leaving the sequence unchanged) in such a way that in the resulting sequence AAA, the sum of the values of bitwise AND for all prefixes of the sequence, i.e. ‚àëi=1NAND(A1,A2,‚Ä¶Ai)\sum_{i=1}^N AND(A_1, A_2, \ldots A_i)‚àëi=1N‚ÄãAND(A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶Ai‚Äã), is maximum possible.

Find the maximum value of this sum.
constraints: 0‚â§Ai<N0 \leq A_i \lt N0‚â§Ai‚Äã<N for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2202^{20}220
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the maximum possible value of the sum of values of bitwise AND for all prefixes.
